hypotheses_tested.description: FYE month is in {2,3} (retailer convention) for SAFE; varies for UNSAFE
hypotheses_tested.rationale: SAFE_FYE distribution: {2: 16, 1: 2}; UNSAFE_FYE distribution: {2: 4, 1: 3, 4: 1, 3: 1, None: 1, 7: 1}. CNM/PVH/PLCE/DKS UNSAFE all FYE=2 — overlap with SAFE. LOW/RH SAFE FYE=1 — overlap with UNSAFE.

hypotheses_tested.description: On all PRIOR 10-Q rows: cal_year(prior_period_of_report) - prior_xbrl_DocumentFiscalYearFocus must be 0. If ANY prior 10-Q has delta != 0, the issuer's XBRL uses year-of-start convention (or has data quality issues).
hypotheses_tested.rationale: ZERO false positives — every SAFE ticker has all-zero 10-Q deltas. Catches BOX/NTAP/NTNX/WDAY/WMS as UNSAFE_PRED. MISSES GIII (true year_of_start in EX-99.1, year_of_end in XBRL — divergence invisible from XBRL) and 5 audit-error tickers (ANF/PVH/CNM/PLCE/DKS) where the 'wrong' classifications are largely audit-truth-label errors, not real structural issues.

hypotheses_tested.description: SAFE = 52/53-week filer (period_end day-of-month varies); UNSAFE = month-end filer (period_end is last day of month).
hypotheses_tested.rationale: ROST is 52/53wk SAFE but profile.csv shape says month_end (small N=2). ANF/CNM/DKS/PVH/PLCE are 52/53wk UNSAFE. Strong correlation but not clean — 5 UNSAFE tickers are 52/53wk.

hypotheses_tested.description: Goal 6f's MULTI_PRIOR_STABLE_OFFSET intent: across all PRIOR 10-Q rows, delta = sec_truth_FY - prior_xbrl_FY must be constant (and equal to 0 if year_of_end announcement convention).
hypotheses_tested.rationale: This requires sec_truth_FY which depends on EX-99.1 parsing — not available in production runtime. It catches GIII (delta=1 stable) and BOX/NTAP/NTNX/WDAY/WMS, but it's not a PIT-safe production signal. Demonstrates the underlying separation but cannot ship.

hypotheses_tested.computed_signal_per_ticker: Not separately computed — cadence info not in pit_prior_features.csv at usable granularity
hypotheses_tested.rationale: Cadence between subsequent 8-Ks for SAFE 52/53wk and UNSAFE month-end filers might differ, but this overlaps with H3 which is rejected. Not pursued.

hypotheses_tested.description: Registrant DEI-derived FYE month vs XBRL FY year-of-start vs year-of-end. Detect divergence between announcement convention (EX-99.1) and XBRL convention.
hypotheses_tested.computed_signal_per_ticker: GIII has 10-Q XBRL FY = cal_year(pof) (year_of_end) but EX-99.1 announces year_of_start. From XBRL alone, GIII looks identical to SAFE issuers.
hypotheses_tested.rationale: The structural information that distinguishes GIII from SAFE issuers lives in EX-99.1 text, not in XBRL DEI. GIII's prior 10-Q XBRL fields are indistinguishable from a year_of_end SAFE issuer's. This divergence is structurally invisible to PIT-allowed signals.

hypotheses_tested.description: Stale XBRL FY: |cal_year(prior_pof) - prior_xbrl_fy| >= 2 indicates stale/buggy XBRL — flag as untrustworthy.
hypotheses_tested.computed_signal_per_ticker: Only 1 row across 408: CNM 8K=0001856525-25-000115 has prior 10-K period_of_report=2025-02-02 with prior_xbrl_fy=2023 (diff=2).
hypotheses_tested.rationale: This catches 1 specific data-quality wrong (CNM's stale 10-K XBRL FY) and could be added as an XBRL sanity check, but doesn't address the GIII or audit-error wrongs. Worth shipping as a defensive check but not a primary discriminator.

best_separator_caveat: ZERO false positives but 6 false negatives. As a production gate it would safely recover all SAFE tickers without breaking any, while still leaking 22 wrong rows on 6 UNSAFE tickers. Of those 22 wrongs: ~10-12 are audit truth-label errors (back-references in EX-99.1 narrative — DKS/ANF/PVH/PLCE), ~1 is a stale-XBRL data-quality issue (CNM, catchable by H7), and ~12 are GENUINE year_of_start announcement convention with year_of_end XBRL (GIII — structurally invisible).

if_clean_separator.rule_pseudocode: Per-ticker decision: trust XBRL advance only if for ALL prior 10-Qs of this ticker, cal_year(prior_period_of_report) == prior_xbrl_DocumentFiscalYearFocus (delta == 0 across history). If ANY prior 10-Q has delta != 0, fail-closed.
if_clean_separator.ship_verdict: SHIP-WITH-LIMITS — H2 is a clean SAFE-side gate (no FPs across the 18 SAFE tickers). The 6 UNSAFE tickers it doesn't catch (GIII + 5 audit-error) require either ticker tables (banned) or EX-99.1 parsing (banned). 22 new wrongs in exchange for 243 recovers might be acceptable; alternatively layer on H7 (stale-XBRL detector) and consider adding GIII as a known structural exception (still requires a ticker exception).

if_no_clean_separator: MULTI_PRIOR_STABLE_OFFSET (Goal 6f's H4-equivalent that uses sec_truth) had 158 correct + 17 wrong on Tier A+B because it relies on truth-FY which is not PIT-available. The 17 wrongs concentrate on: GIII (10 — genuine year_of_start announcement vs year_of_end XBRL), DKS (4 — audit truth-label data errors), PVH/ANF/PLCE/PHM (1 each — mix of audit errors and stale XBRL). The residual class is exactly the divergence between EX-99.1 announcement convention and XBRL DEI DocumentFiscalYearFocus. Whenever an issuer chooses different conventions for these two surfaces, no XBRL-only signal can detect the divergence — the information lives only in EX-99.1 text.
verdict_rationale: H2 (XBRL prior 10-Q delta == 0 across history) is a CLEAN SAFE-side gate: 18/18 TN, 0/18 FP. It catches 5 of 11 UNSAFE tickers cleanly (BOX/NTAP/NTNX/WDAY/WMS). The remaining 6 UNSAFE tickers either (a) have audit truth-label errors in the eval set (ANF/DKS/PVH/CNM/PLCE — 10 of 22 wrongs are non-structural), or (b) carry a structural XBRL/announcement-convention divergence that PIT-allowed signals cannot detect (GIII — 12 wrongs).
data_pipeline_lead: If a future data layer materializes EX-99.1 cover page parse (or DEI element from cover page like dei:CoverFiscalYearFocus on EX-99 carrier docs), retest H6 with announcement-vs-XBRL divergence detection. Specifically: any element/text indicator that the issuer's EX-99.1 announcement uses year_of_start while XBRL uses year_of_end. Naming: GIII_DIVERGENCE_DETECTOR. Until that data exists, the GIII class is empirically irreducible under current locks.
